canonical_definition: Based on the available data, RLBench is characterized as a simulated benchmark for robot manipulation. It is specifically described as a "simulator of a Franka robot performing tabletop manipulation for a wide array of both prehensile and non-prehensile tasks" (HAMSTER: Hierarchical Action Models for Open-World Robot Manipulation). The single provided source uses this benchmark for two purposes: to generate trajectories and to evaluate manipulation-related path prediction. The paper details its use for generating a large dataset, specifically "1000 episodes for each of 81 robot manipulation tasks." Within this context, the instrument is referred to as a "popular robot manipulation benchmark."
papers: BadRobot: Jailbreaking Embodied LLM Agents in the Physical World
HAMSTER: Hierarchical Action Models for Open-World Robot Manipulation
NeSyC: A Neuro-symbolic Continual Learner For Complex Embodied Tasks in Open Domains

definitions.text: RLBench is a simulated robot manipulation benchmark with tabletop tasks used here to generate trajectories and evaluate manipulation-related path prediction.
definitions.paper: HAMSTER: Hierarchical Action Models for Open-World Robot Manipulation
